Meta CTO’s message to engineers and college graduates looking to join technology industry
description: Meta's CTO, Andrew Bosworth, urged aspiring tech professionals to prioritize hands-on building over theoretical study. He emphasized immersing oneself in practical projects, from "vibe coding" with AI tools for software to using Raspberry Pi and Arduino for hardware.  Bosworth advocates for continuous experimentation and action to develop essential skills for the evolving tech landscape.

description: A horrific incident unfolded in Patna where a 22-year-old man allegedly gang-raped his three-year-old niece with two friends. The child's cries alerted locals, leading to the accused fleeing. Police swiftly apprehended two suspects, including the uncle, and are pursuing the third. The injured toddler is receiving medical care.

His heart rate dropped to 40: How a 68-year-old survived a life-threatening condition
description: Shivmurthi, 68, battled severe health issues including a recurrent hiatus hernia and a dangerously low heart rate. Doctors first stabilized his heart with a pacemaker. He then received a feeding tube to manage digestive problems. Finally, a complex robotic surgery successfully repaired the hernia. Shivmurthi is now recovering well, regaining weight and enjoying a more normal life.

title: Amid job cuts, Canadian billionaire Kevin O’Leary points out a good thing about AI layoffs
description: Kevin O'Leary, the Canadian billionaire, asserts that artificial intelligence is elevating human creativity, making artists and storytellers more valuable than ever. He highlights how compelling social media content, especially short-form videos, now commands significant salaries due to measurable customer acquisition. O'Leary believes AI is a powerful productivity tool, fostering new industries and opportunities across all economic sectors.

title: KVS admission result 2026: When will the first provisional list release? Check here
description: With registrations for KVS admissions 2026–27 now concluded, students and parents are awaiting the release of provisional selection lists scheduled in April. The first list for Balvatika classes will be declared on April 8, followed by Class I on April 9, with slight changes in select states. Additional lists are planned later in the month if seats remain vacant. Admissions for other classes and offline processes will also proceed through April as per the notified schedule.
